mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate employment income, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean 20-day forward return following positive PAYEMS momentum is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% increases the cost of capital for corporations and households, reducing present values of future cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, positive 60-day forward returns occur more than 45% of the time, or the mean return in triggered months is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate above 5.0% and a greater than 10% decline in housing starts over six months signals simultaneous credit contraction and construction sector stress, which amplifies downside risk to the broader economy and jointly drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% AND HOUST declines > 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant in a bivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicate an inflection point in manufacturing activity and corporate profit growth, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, two-month INDPRO declines coincide with positive 5-day forward returns more than 50% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized signals broad demand stagnation that compresses corporate revenue visibility and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over three months signals accelerating job losses and collapsing household income expectations, which reduces equity risk appetite and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The triggered signal coincides with negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer prices alongside an unchanged or lower federal funds rate generates a negative real policy rate shock that squeezes corporate margins and sparks fears of reactive tightening, jointly producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.4% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower
failure_condition: The interaction term does not improve directional accuracy over a main-effects-only model as measured by a Diebold-Mariano test (p > 0.10), or the joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and operating leverage that boosts corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO momentum remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive momentum fails to coincide with positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months.
